description_long: This shrub features slender, upright stems with clusters of palmate leaves, each leaf composed of several elongated, rounded leaflets radiating from a central point. The foliage is a mix of medium to dark green, with some leaves showing lighter green or yellowish variegation along the edges. The plant has an open, airy structure, with branches spreading outward and upward, giving it a delicate, layered appearance. There are no visible flowers or fruit, and the stems appear thin and flexible. The shrub is relatively low to the ground, with a maximum height of about half a meter, and its spread is slightly wider than its height. This type of shrub is commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or landscaped areas where a low-maintenance, decorative plant is desired.
description: A low, airy shrub with slender stems and palmate, variegated green leaves arranged in open clusters. The plant has a delicate, layered structure and no visible flowers.
description_view_0: Two main clusters of palmate leaves are visible, with stems spreading horizontally and leaves showing variegated green tones.
description_view_1: The shrub appears more vertical, with overlapping leaves and a clear view of the layered, open branching structure.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, this angle highlights the horizontal spread and separation between the two main clusters of foliage.
description_view_3: A more vertical perspective, emphasizing the density of the lower leaves and the upright growth of the stems.
